summary.intent: Implement task #284: the agnostic core state machine for a DateRangeField component in a Rust UI component library, following an existing spec and codebase conventions.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented `DateRangeField` as a stateful composition of two `DateField` instances with range coordination, 100% line/function coverage, all 10 xci-fast CI gates passing, spec synced to match implementation, and PR committed and pushed.

repo: Components use a Machine trait with `connect` method that returns an `Api` struct; child component config flows through props methods like `start_field_props()`/`end_field_props()` rather than embedding children in the `Part` enum (matching date_picker/date_time_picker patterns).
Multi-arg MessageFn closures trigger a clippy type-complexity lint; the convention is to define a named type alias (e.g., `type RangeDescriptionFn = MessageFn<(CalendarDate, CalendarDate), String>`).
The snapshot-count gate auto-detects components by snapshot filename and enforces per-component minimums; adding 18 snapshots for date_range_field passed automatically.
Unreferenced snapshot check (`insta --unreferenced=reject`) requires `--features i18n` to avoid false positives from the pre-existing `utility/highlight` component tests gated on that feature.
No date_time modules use `# Examples` doctests — the convention is integration tests only.
The `DateRange::normalized()` method returns `Option` (not the range directly), and `DateRange::new` requires `u8`/`Result` construction — the spec had used incorrect API shapes.
`intl_backend` comes from `Env` (not Props/Context), so `range_description` locale formatting must wire through `Env` on the Context, which the spec had omitted.
The catalog (parity table) is a priority/parity reference, not an implementation tracker — no update needed when implementing an agnostic core.
Adapter specs for DateRangeField already existed and used `api.start_field_props()`, confirming the design approach before implementation started.

code.finding: Context must track `start_date: Option<CalendarDate>` and `end_date: Option<CalendarDate>` independently; `value: Option<DateRange>` must be derived from them. The spec's `Option<DateRange>` on Context cannot hold a partial (one-sided) range, making it impossible to complete a range from two separate StartValueChange/EndValueChange events.
Readonly guard on StartValueChange/EndValueChange event handlers was initially untested — discovered via llvm-cov coverage report.
Empty start/end separate hidden inputs (when value is None) were uncovered — discovered via llvm-cov.

workflow: Running `cargo xci-fast` as a pre-push gate (10 checks including clippy, conformance, proptest, snapshot-count, spec validate, snippet compile, doc build) before committing prevents CI failures.
The 3-phase post-implementation audit (spec/impl drift → missing coverage → test gaps) caught a real issue (unused `Api::new`) and 3 coverage gaps that unit tests hadn't exercised.
llvm-cov per-file coverage with `--show-missing-lines` is the right tool for finding uncovered branches after initial test writing.
Two consecutive clean audit rounds serves as the convergence criterion for the 'anything missing?' phase.
Spawning parallel Explore agents to read DateField, DateRange shared types, and recently-added range components simultaneously saved significant time during the initial codebase exploration.

friction: The spec had 8 API-drift issues vs the actual Rust codebase (wrong method names like `min`/`max` vs `min_value`/`max_value`, wrong return types, missing Context fields, incorrect date construction APIs) requiring a verification pass before planning.
`SendResult` field name was `state_changed` not `transitioned` — required reading core lib source to discover.
The `awk` command in the coverage check leaked into other files' output, requiring a targeted per-file coverage command instead.
The `insta unreferenced` check aborted (false positive) without `--features i18n`, requiring a follow-up run with the feature flag.
The `ExitPlanMode` tool required a `ToolSearch` call to load its schema before it could be invoked.
Multiple sequential read passes on `date_field/mod.rs` were needed to extract different sections (Props, child-composition pattern, ARIA emission) because the file was too large to read in one pass.
open_items: Adapter implementations (framework-specific bindings) for DateRangeField are not yet written — adapter specs exist but the agnostic core is the only deliverable for #284.
The `DateRange::normalized()→None` guard (3 missed coverage regions) is intentionally untested — it's a defensive guard for a calendar-mismatch scenario unreachable in single-calendar use.
The `utility/highlight` component has pre-existing unreferenced snapshots when run without `--features i18n` — a known pre-existing issue unrelated to this PR.
The `block v0.1.6` transitive-dependency clippy note is a pre-existing crate-wide warning, not introduced by this PR.
